query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c5ec2c6a).

## Issue to Resolve

Spacing around `..<` should be identical to spacing around `..`
Given code below with inconsistent spacing around `..<`:
```
@OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
fun foo(int: Int): String =
    when (int) {
        in 10..<20 -> "A"
        in 20..< 30 -> "B"
        in 30 ..< 40 -> "C"
        in 40 ..<50 -> "D"
        else -> "unknown"
    }
```

should be formatted as:
```
@OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
fun foo(int: Int): String =
    when (int) {
        in 10..<20 -> "A"
        in 20..<30 -> "B"
        in 30..<40 -> "C"
        in 40..<50 -> "D"
        else -> "unknown"
    }
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ElementType.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52→
 53→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 54→
 55→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 56→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 59→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
 60→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
 61→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
 62→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
 63→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
 64→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
 65→
 66→
 67→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
 68→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 69→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
 70→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
 71→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
 72→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
 73→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
 74→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
 75→
 76→Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeFile` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromFile`. Likewise, class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeSnippet` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromSnippet`.
 77→
 78→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
 79→
 80→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`                           |
 81→|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 82→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2              | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3                   |
 83→
 84→
 85→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`:
 86→
 87→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`   | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`     |
 88→|-------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
 89→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtlintVersion   | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.KtlintVersion |
 90→
 91→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-logger`:
 92→
 93→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`               | New class/package name in `ktlint-logger`                   |
 94→|-------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
 95→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt | com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt |
 96→
 97→Module `ktlint` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli`. Classes below have been relocated in this module:
 98→
 99→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli` |
100→|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
 5→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
 6→
 7→class SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRuleTest {
 8→    private val spacingAroundRangeOperatorRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRule() }
 9→
10→    @Test
11→    fun `Given a range`() {
12→        val code =
13→            """
14→            val foo1 = (1..12 step 2).last
15→            val foo2 = (1.. 12 step 2).last
16→            val foo3 = (1 .. 12 step 2).last
17→            val foo4 = (1 ..12 step 2).last
18→            fun foo() {
19→                for (i in 1..4) print(i)
20→            }
21→            """.trimIndent()
22→        val formattedCode =
23→            """
24→            val foo1 = (1..12 step 2).last
25→            val foo2 = (1..12 step 2).last
26→            val foo3 = (1..12 step 2).last
27→            val foo4 = (1..12 step 2).last
28→            fun foo() {
29→                for (i in 1..4) print(i)
30→            }
31→            """.trimIndent()
32→        spacingAroundRangeOperatorRuleAssertThat(code)
33→            .hasLintViolations(
34→                LintViolation(2, 16, "Unexpected spacing after \"..\""),
35→                LintViolation(3, 15, "Unexpected spacing around \"..\""),
36→                LintViolation(4, 14, "Unexpected spacing before \"..\""),
37→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
38→    }
39→
40→    @Test
41→    fun `Given a range in a for loop`() {
42→        val code =
43→            """
44→            fun foo() {
45→                for (i in 1..4) print(i)
46→                for (i in 1.. 4) print(i)
47→                for (i in 1 .. 4) print(i)
48→                for (i in 1 ..4) print(i)
49→            }
50→            """.trimIndent()
51→        val formattedCode =
52→            """
53→            fun foo() {
54→                for (i in 1..4) print(i)
55→                for (i in 1..4) print(i)
56→                for (i in 1..4) print(i)
57→                for (i in 1..4) print(i)
58→            }
59→            """.trimIndent()
60→        spacingAroundRangeOperatorRuleAssertThat(code)

 80→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 81→
 82→/**
 83→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 84→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 85→ *
 86→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 87→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 88→ * indentation rule is not run.
 89→ */
 90→public class WrappingRule :
 91→    StandardRule(
 92→        id = "wrapping",
 93→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 94→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 95→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 96→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 97→        ),
 98→    ) {
 99→    private var line = 1
100→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
101→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
102→
103→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
104→        line = 1
105→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
106→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
107→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
108→        )
109→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
110→    }
111→
112→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
113→        node: ASTNode,
114→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→    ) {
117→        when (node.elementType) {
118→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
119→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
120→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
121→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
122→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
123→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
124→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
125→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
126→        }
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
130→        node: ASTNode,
131→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133→    ) {
134→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
135→
136→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
137→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
138→            return
139→        }
140→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
141→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
142→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
143→        val wrapBlock =
144→            when {
145→                startOfBlock.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) -> {
146→                    // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
147→                    false
148→                }
149→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
150→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
151→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
152→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
153→                }
154→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
155→                    val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
156→                        node
157→                            .leaves(false)
158→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
159→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
160→                    val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
161→                        node
162→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
163→                            .leavesIncludingSelf()
164→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
165→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
166→                    lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength
167→                }
168→                else -> false
169→            }
170→        if (wrapBlock) {
171→            startOfBlock
172→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
173→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
174→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
175→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
176→                        autoCorrect,
177→                        emit,
178→                    )
179→                }
180→        }
181→    }
182→
183→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
184→        node: ASTNode,
185→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
186→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
187→    ) {
188→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
189→        var newlineInBetween = false
190→        var parameterListInBetween = false
191→        var numberOfArgs = 0
192→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
193→        // matching ), ] or }
194→        val r = node.nextSibling {
195→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
196→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
197→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
198→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
199→            if (isValueArgument) {
200→                numberOfArgs++
201→                firstArg = it
202→            }
203→            it.elementType == rElementType
204→        }!!
205→        if (
206→            !newlineInBetween ||
207→            // keep { p ->
208→            // }
209→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
210→            // keep ({
211→            // }) and (object : C {
212→            // })
213→            (
214→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
215→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
216→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
217→                )
218→        ) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
222→            // keep:
223→            // for (foo in listOf(
224→            //     "foo-1",
225→            //     "foo-2"
226→            // )) { ... }
227→            // but reject:
228→            // for (
229→            //     foo in listOf(
230→            //         "foo-1",
231→            //         "foo-2"
232→            //     )
233→            // ) { ... }
234→            return
235→        }
236→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
237→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
238→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
239→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
240→                    it.textLength > 0
241→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
242→            // IDEA quirk:
243→            // if (true &&
244→            //     true
245→            // ) {
246→            // }
247→            // instead of
248→            // if (
249→            //     true &&
250→            //     true
251→            // ) {
252→            // }
253→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
254→        ) {
255→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
256→        }
257→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
258→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.indent())
259→        }
260→    }
261→
262→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
263→        node: ASTNode,
264→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→    ) {
267→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
268→        if (
269→            node.textContains('\n') &&
270→            entries.size > 1 &&
271→            // e.g.
272→            //
273→            // class A : B, C,
274→            //     D
275→            // or
276→            // class A : B, C({
277→            // }), D
278→            //
279→            // but not
280→            //
281→            // class A : B, C, D({
282→            // })
283→            !(
284→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
285→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
286→                )
287→        ) {
288→            // put space after :
289→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
290→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
291→                if (
292→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
293→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
294→                ) {
295→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
296→                }
297→            }
298→            // put entries on separate lines
299→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
300→            for (c in node.children()) {
301→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
302→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
303→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
304→                ) {
305→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
306→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
307→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
308→                        emit = emit,
309→                        indent = node.indent(),
310→                    )
311→                }
312→            }
313→        }
314→    }
315→
316→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
317→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
318→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
319→
320→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
321→        node: ASTNode,
322→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
323→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
324→    ) {
325→        for (c in node.children()) {
326→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
327→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
328→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
329→                else -> false
330→            }
331→            if (hasLineBreak) {
332→                // rearrange
333→                //
334→                // a, b, value(
335→                // ), c, d
336→                //
337→                // to
338→                //
339→                // a, b,
340→                // value(
341→                // ),
342→                // c, d
343→
344→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
345→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
346→                if (
347→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
348→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
349→                ) {
350→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
351→                }
352→                // insert \n after multi-line value
353→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
354→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
355→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
356→                if (
357→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
358→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
359→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
360→                    // value(
361→                    // ), // a comment
362→                    // c, d
363→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
364→                ) {
365→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
366→                }
367→            }
368→        }
369→    }
370→
371→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
372→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
373→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
374→
375→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
376→        node: ASTNode,
377→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
378→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
379→    ) {
380→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
381→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
382→            node
383→                .children()
384→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
385→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
386→                    typeProjection
387→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
388→                        .let { prevSibling ->
389→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
390→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
391→                                if (autoCorrect) {
392→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
393→                                        node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
394→                                    )
395→                                }
396→                            }
397→                        }
398→                }
399→
400→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
401→            node
402→                .findChildByType(GT)
403→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
404→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
405→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
406→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
407→                        if (autoCorrect) {
408→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
409→                                node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
410→                            )
411→                        }
412→                    }
413→                }
414→
415→            Unit
416→        }
417→    }
418→
419→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
420→        node: ASTNode,
421→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
422→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
423→    ) {
424→        node
425→            .treeParent
426→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
427→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
428→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
429→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
430→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
431→            ?.let {
432→                // rewriting
433→                // """
434→                //     text
435→                // _""".trimIndent()
436→                // to
437→                // """
438→                //     text
439→                // _
440→                // """.trimIndent()
441→                emit(
442→                    node.startOffset,
443→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
444→                    true,
445→                )
446→                if (autoCorrect) {
447→                    node as LeafPsiElement
448→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
449→                }
450→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
451→            }
452→    }
453→
454→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
455→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
456→        // if token is in lTokenSet
457→        //     find matching rToken
458→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
459→        // return false
460→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
461→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
462→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
463→            // special cases:
464→            // x = y.f({ z ->
465→            // })
466→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
467→            // 2, 3)
468→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
469→        }
470→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
471→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
472→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
473→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
474→        }
475→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
476→            return true
477→        }
478→        return false
479→    }
480→
481→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
482→        node: ASTNode,
483→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
484→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
485→    ) {
486→        val p = node.treeParent
487→        if (
488→            // check
489→            // `{ p -> ... }`
490→            // and
491→            // `when { m -> ... }`
492→            // only
493→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
494→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
495→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
496→            // permit
497→            // when {
498→            //     m -> 0 + d({
499→            //     })
500→            // }
501→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
502→            // permit
503→            // when (this) {
504→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
505→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
506→            //     -> true
507→            // }
508→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
509→        ) {
510→            return
511→        }
512→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
513→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
514→        }
515→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
516→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
517→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
518→        }
519→    }
520→
521→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
522→        node: ASTNode,
523→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
524→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
525→        indent: String,
526→    ) {
527→        emit(
528→            node.startOffset - 1,
529→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
530→            true,
531→        )
532→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
533→        if (autoCorrect) {
534→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
535→        }
536→    }
537→
538→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
539→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
540→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
541→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
542→        indent: String? = null,
543→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
544→    ) {
545→        emit(
546→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
547→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
548→            true,
549→        )
550→        LOGGER.trace {
551→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
552→        }
553→        if (autoCorrect) {
554→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
555→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
556→        }
557→    }
558→
559→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
560→        for (child in node.children()) {
561→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
562→                val v = child.text
563→                if (v == "\n") {
564→                    return true
565→                }
566→            }
567→        }
568→        return false
569→    }
570→
571→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
572→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
573→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
574→            textContains('\n')
575→        } else {
576→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
577→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
578→        }
579→    }
580→
581→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
582→
583→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
584→
585→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
586→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
587→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
588→
589→    /**
590→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
591→     *     for (foo in listOf(
592→     *         "foo-1",
593→     *         "foo-2"
594→     *     )) { ... }
595→     * but reject:
596→     *     for (
597→     *         foo in listOf(
598→     *             "foo-1",
599→     *             "foo-2"
600→     *         )
601→     *     ) { ... }
602→     */
603→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
604→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
605→            return false
606→        }
607→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
608→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
609→        }
610→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
611→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
612→        }
613→
614→        var node: ASTNode? = this
615→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
616→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
617→                return false
618→            }
619→            node = node.nextSibling { true }
620→        }
621→        return true
622→    }
623→
624→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
625→        node: ASTNode,
626→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
627→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
628→    ) {
629→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
630→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
631→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
632→                return
633→            }
634→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
635→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
636→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
637→            val wrapBlock =
638→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
639→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
640→                    )
641→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
642→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
643→                    endOfBlock,
644→                    autoCorrect,
645→                    emit,
646→                    node.treeParent.indent(),
647→                )
648→            }
649→        }
650→    }
651→
652→    private companion object {
653→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
654→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
655→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
656→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
657→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
658→            ).toMap()
659→    }
660→}
661→
662→public val WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = WrappingRule().ruleId
663→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
200→
201→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
202→
203→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
204→
205→```kotlin
206→public class SomeRule : Rule(
207→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
208→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
209→) {
210→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
211→
212→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
213→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
214→  }
215→  
216→  ...
217→}
218→```
219→
220→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
221→
222→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
223→
224→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
225→
226→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
227→
228→### Added
229→
230→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
231→* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
232→* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
233→* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
234→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
235→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
236→
237→### Removed
238→
239→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
240→
241→### Fixed
242→
243→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
244→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
245→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
246→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
247→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
248→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
249→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 8→
 9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
240→
241→### Fixed
242→
243→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
244→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
245→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
246→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
247→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
248→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
249→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))
250→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824))
251→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
252→* A newline before a function return type should not be removed in case that leads to exceeding the maximum line length `function-return-type-spacing` ([#1764](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1764))
253→* Wrap annotations on type arguments in same way as with other constructs `annotation`, `wrapping` ([#1725](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1725))
254→* Fix indentation of try-catch-finally when catch or finally starts on a newline `indent` ([#1788](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1788))
255→* Fix indentation of a multiline typealias `indent` ([#1788](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1788))
256→* Fix false positive when multiple KDOCs exists between a declaration and another annotated declaration `spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations` ([#1802](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1802))
257→* Fix false positive when a single line statement containing a block having exactly the maximum line length is preceded by a blank line `wrapping` ([#1808](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1808))
258→* Fix false positive when a single line contains multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expressions `indent` ([#1830](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1830))
259→* When `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout` contains a `|` but no import exists that match any pattern before the first `|` then do not report a violation nor insert a blank line `import-ordering` ([#1845](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1845))
260→
261→### Changed
262→* Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).
263→  Changelog:
264→* KtLint CLI exits with an error in any of following cases (this list is not exhaustive):
265→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated RuleSetProviderV2.
266→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required RuleSetProviderV3.
267→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated ReporterProvider.
268→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required ReporterProviderV2.
269→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
270→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20-Beta` and Kotlin version to `1.8.10`.
271→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1787](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
272→* For the new code style `ktlint_official`, do not allow wildcard imports `java.util` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic` by default. Important: `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` needs to be set to value `unset` in order to enforce IntelliJ IDEA default formatter to not generate wildcard imports `no-wildcard-imports` ([#1797](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1797))
273→
274→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
275→
276→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
277→
278→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
279→
280→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
281→
282→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
283→
284→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
285→```editorconfig
286→[*.{kt,kts}]
287→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
288→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
289→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
290→```
291→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
292→
293→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
294→
295→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
296→```
297→KtLintRuleEngine(
298→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
299→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
300→        EditorConfig
301→            .builder()
302→            .section(
303→                Section
304→                    .builder()
305→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
306→                    .properties(
307→                        Property
308→                            .builder()
309→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
310→                            .value("enabled"),
311→                    ),
312→            )
313→            .build()
314→    )
315→)
316→```
317→If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
318→
319→If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).
320→
321→### Added
322→
323→### Removed
324→
325→### Fixed
326→* Fix with array-syntax annotations on the same line as other annotations `annotation` ([#1765](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1765))
327→* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
328→* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
329→* Allow diacritics in names of classes, functions packages, and properties `class-naming`, `function-naming`, `package-name`, `property-naming` ([#1757](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1757))
330→* Prevent violation of `file-name` rule on code snippets ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
331→* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
332→* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))
333→
334→### Changed
335→

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 8→
 9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
13→
14→### Fixed
15→- Spacing around rangeUntil operator (..<) should be identical to spacing around range operator (..) `range-spacing`
16→
17→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
18→
19→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
20→
21→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
22→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
23→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
24→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
25→
26→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
27→
28→#### Experimental rules
29→
30→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
31→
32→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
33→
34→#### EditorConfig
35→
36→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
37→
38→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
39→
40→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
41→
42→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
43→
44→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
45→
46→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
47→
48→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
49→
50→#### Package restructuring and class relocation

content.text:
I have implemented a fix for the spacing around the `..<` (rangeUntil) operator to make it consistent with the spacing around the `..` (range) operator.

### Changes:
1.  **Added `RANGE_UNTIL` to `ElementType`**:
    *   Updated `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ElementType.kt` to include `RANGE_UNTIL` mapped to `KtTokens.RANGE_UNTIL`.
    *   Updated `ktlint-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/core/ast/ElementType.kt` for backward compatibility.
2.  **Updated `SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRule`**:
    *   Modified `SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRule.kt` to handle both `RANGE` and `RANGE_UNTIL` element types.
    *   The rule now uses the actual operator text (`..` or `..<`) in error messages instead of a hardcoded string.
3.  **Updated `NoUnusedImportsRule`**:
    *   Added `rangeUntil` to the `OPERATOR_SET` in `NoUnusedImportsRule.kt` to prevent it from being marked as an unused import when used as an operator.
4.  **Updated `CHANGELOG.md`**:
    *   Added an entry under the `Unreleased` section for this fix.

### Verification:
I created a new test file `SpacingAroundRangeUntilOperatorRuleTest.kt` with several test cases covering inconsistent spacing before, after, and around the `..<` operator. The tests confirmed that the rule now correctly identifies and fixes these issues. I also ran all existing tests in the `ktlint-ruleset-standard` module to ensure no regressions were introduced.

```kotlin
@OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
fun foo(int: Int): String =
    when (int) {
        in 10..<20 -> "A"
        in 20..< 30 -> "B"   // Unexpected spacing after "..<"
        in 30 ..< 40 -> "C"  // Unexpected spacing around "..<"
        in 40 ..<50 -> "D"   // Unexpected spacing before "..<"
        else -> "unknown"
    }
```
Now correctly formats to:
```kotlin
@OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
fun foo(int: Int): String =
    when (int) {
        in 10..<20 -> "A"
        in 20..<30 -> "B"
        in 30..<40 -> "C"
        in 40..<50 -> "D"
        else -> "unknown"
    }
```

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
